name: King Jamshid, legendary Persian monarch
shortDescription: Jamshid is a legendary king of Persia in Persian mythology. He is credited with bringing prosperity and civilization to his people, but his hubris led to his downfall.
description: Jamshid is a prominent figure in Persian mythology and folklore, often depicted as the fourth king of the Pishdadian dynasty. He is credited with bringing numerous advancements to humanity, including the weaving of cloth, the mining of precious stones, the construction of dwellings, and the creation of perfumes and wines. Jamshid is also said to have divided society into four classes: priests, warriors, farmers, and artisans. During his reign, the world experienced a golden age of peace and abundance. However, Jamshid's hubris (khwarrah) grew with his power and success. He declared himself a god, alienating the divine and his people. This act of arrogance led to the loss of his divine grace (farrah) and opened the way for the tyrannical rule of Zahhak. Jamshid was eventually overthrown and met a gruesome end, sawed in half by Zahhak. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of pride and the importance of humility.
quests: Brought civilization and prosperity to Persia
Built a Vara to escape a devastating winter
abilities: Possessed the Cup of Jamshid (a divining cup)

sources.title: History of al-Tabari, Vol. 1: From the Creation to the Flood
sources.author: al-Tabari
